summary: Gruber responds to Steven Frank of Panic's frustration with VersionTracker user reviews, where impatient and unreasonable users threaten developers with bad reviews instead of reporting bugs directly. He argues that anonymous review forums like VersionTracker's have zero credibility due to anonymity, poor writing, technical inaccuracies, and a fixation on price rather than quality. He notes the irony that such abusive users only harm good developers who actually try to be responsive, while bad developers remain unaffected. Gruber concludes that while VersionTracker is useful as a software listing service, the review feature actively damages its brand and should be removed entirely.
key_points: Users who threaten developers with bad reviews instead of reporting bugs through proper channels only punish responsive developers while leaving bad ones unscathed
There are only two reasonable responses to software bugs: report them to the developer or keep them to yourself — publicly lambasting software without first contacting the developer is unreasonable
Real criticism in any field depends entirely on credibility, which anonymous review forums fundamentally lack
VersionTracker reviews are undermined not just by anonymity but by poor writing, technical inaccuracies, and an obsessive focus on price
VersionTracker's value is narrowly focused on being a comprehensive software listing — the review feature detracts from that brand
money_quotes: They only annoy the good guys: developers who are actually trying to be responsive to their users. The shitty developers who ignore their users aren't bothered, since, well, they ignore their users.
If you're going to take the time to write a report about the bug, why post it to the VersionTracker forums without having first emailed a report to the developer?
Is the goal to see the software actually improve, or just to get off on pissing all over someone else's work?
Everyone knows that marketing is usually bullshit — only the gullible believe unverified marketing information.
Real criticism — whether it be for software, cars, books, or movies — hinges upon but a single thing: credibility.
There is no credibility whatsoever in VersionTracker's anonymous review forums.
That the user reviews are so uniformly bad detracts from the VersionTracker brand.
key_insight: Anonymous user review forums undermine legitimate criticism because real criticism requires credibility, and platforms that host uniformly terrible reviews only damage their own brand while punishing the developers who actually care.
